title: SliceObservation (Live-Verify raw facts)
description: One fresh verifier's RAW FACTS about a slice. NO verdict field — the harness decides 'resolved' by consensus COUNT across the panel, and the calling-session guardrail re-runs the evidence deterministically (this report is advisory, an LLM self-report). This is the disposition-verdict boundary the drive-loop reads from.

test_command.description: The per-slice command actually run (must match the slice's; whole-island runs are a protocol violation).

pass.description: Passing test count reported by the runner.

fail.description: Failing test count reported by the runner.

evidence.description: REFERENCES only (path or content hash + short preview), re-runnable/re-hashable by the calling-session guardrail. Never inline dumps.

ref.description: file path or content hash the guardrail can re-execute/re-hash

observed_application_evidence.description: Confirmation the improvement is reached through the LIVE path (reachable entrypoint + real effect), not a unit-isolated call. The 'observed applied' facet of §5.8.

orphan_free.description: REQUIRED. false if any added symbol is unwired/unreachable (§5.8 wire-or-drop). A missing value must not silently pass — hence required, not optional.

discovered.description: Found items. BACKLOG-ONLY — never turned into a drivable slice (no test_command/oracle) and never grows the frozen denominator.

change_kind.description: intent = an existing AC's meaning or live-path expectation changes → forces user-owned escalation (no method-laundering).
